audio.h(3) Library Functions Manual audio.h(3)

NAME

audio.h - Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct audio_batch_info_t
struct audio_frame_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t audio_parse_batch_header (const void *data, size_t len, audio_batch_info_t *out_batch)
asciichat_error_t audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t *batch)
bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Detailed Description

Stub audio types for WASM builds (uses Web Audio API instead of PortAudio).

This header provides minimal audio type definitions for WASM builds. In WASM, audio is handled by Web Audio API via AudioPipeline.ts instead of PortAudio. This stub provides just enough definitions for network packet parsing to compile.

Definition in file lib/platform/wasm/stubs/audio.h.

Function Documentation

bool audio_is_supported_sample_rate (uint32_t sample_rate)

Definition at line 2010 of file lib/audio/audio.c.

2010                                                           {
2011   // List of commonly supported audio sample rates
2012   static const uint32_t supported_rates[] = {
2013       8000,   // Telephone quality
2014       16000,  // Wideband telephony
2015       24000,  // High quality speech
2016       32000,  // Good for video
2017       44100,  // CD quality (less common in VoIP)
2018       48000,  // Standard professional
2019       96000,  // High-end professional
2020       192000, // Ultra-high-end mastering
2021   };
2022 
2023   const size_t rate_count = sizeof(supported_rates) / sizeof(supported_rates[0]);
2024   for (size_t i = 0; i < rate_count; i++) {
2025     if (sample_rate == supported_rates[i]) {
2026       return true;
2027     }
2028   }
2029 
2030   return false;
2031 }




Referenced by audio_validate_batch_params().






asciichat_error_t
  audio_parse_batch_header (const void * data, size_t len,
  audio_batch_info_t * out_batch)


Definition at line 1944 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1944                                                                                                         {
1945   if (!data) {
1946     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header data pointer is NULL");
1947   }
1948 
1949   if (!out_batch) {
1950     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info output pointer is NULL");
1951   }
1952 
1953   if (len < sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t)) {
1954     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch header too small (len=%zu, expected=%zu)", len,
1955                      sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t));
1956   }
1957 
1958   const audio_batch_packet_t *batch_header = (const audio_batch_packet_t *)data;
1959 
1960   // Unpack network byte order values to host byte order
1961   out_batch->batch_count = ntohl(batch_header->batch_count);
1962   out_batch->total_samples = ntohl(batch_header->total_samples);
1963   out_batch->sample_rate = ntohl(batch_header->sample_rate);
1964   out_batch->channels = ntohl(batch_header->channels);
1965 
1966   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1967 }






References audio_batch_info_t::batch_count,
    audio_batch_info_t::channels, audio_batch_info_t::sample_rate,
    and audio_batch_info_t::total_samples.


Referenced by handle_audio_batch_packet().






asciichat_error_t
  audio_validate_batch_params (const audio_batch_info_t * batch)


Definition at line 1969 of file
  lib/audio/audio.c.


1969                                                                                {
1970   if (!batch) {
1971     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch info pointer is NULL");
1972   }
1973 
1974   // Validate batch_count
1975   if (batch->batch_count == 0) {
1976     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count cannot be zero");
1977   }
1978 
1979   // Check for reasonable max (256 frames per batch is very generous)
1980   if (batch->batch_count > 256) {
1981     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch count too large (batch_count=%u, max=256)", batch->batch_count);
1982   }
1983 
1984   // Validate channels (1=mono, 2=stereo, max 8 for multi-channel)
1985   if (batch->channels == 0 || batch->channels > 8) {
1986     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid channel count (channels=%u, valid=1-8)", batch->channels);
1987   }
1988 
1989   // Validate sample rate
1990   if (!audio_is_supported_sample_rate(batch->sample_rate)) {
1991     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported sample rate (sample_rate=%u)", batch->sample_rate);
1992   }
1993 
1994   // Check for reasonable sample counts
1995   if (batch->total_samples == 0) {
1996     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch has zero samples");
1997   }
1998 
1999   // Each batch typically has samples_per_frame worth of samples
2000   // For 48kHz at 20ms per frame: 48000 * 0.02 = 960 samples per frame
2001   // With max 256 frames, that's up to ~245k samples per batch
2002   if (batch->total_samples > 1000000) {
2003     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Audio batch sample count suspiciously large (total_samples=%u)",
2004                      batch->total_samples);
2005   }
2006 
2007   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2008 }






References audio_is_supported_sample_rate(),
    audio_batch_info_t::batch_count, audio_batch_info_t::channels,
    audio_batch_info_t::sample_rate, and
    audio_batch_info_t::total_samples.
